reason: The statement applies because Josiah is depicted as a servant of the Lord who followed the ways of the Lord with all his heart and soul, as described in the text fragment. He humbled himself before the Lord and sought to fulfill the words of the covenant found in the house of the Lord. Josiah's actions in destroying idolatrous practices and purifying the land align with his role as a servant of God chosen to protect and save the people from divine wrath.
